text.title: HAProxy Production Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in HAProxy production deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Pool stays "all healthy" while users get 502s. Backend processes crashed; an upstream sidecar still answers TCP and returns 502, which HAProxy counts as alive.
antipattern.root-cause: `option httpchk GET /health` is declared without `http-check expect status 200`.
antipattern.fix: Always pair `option httpchk` with `http-check expect status 200` (or a stricter body match). Pre-commit lint rejects any backend missing `expect`.

antipattern.symptom: HAProxy 2.4+ config still uses `nbproc 4`. Stick-tables don't sync between processes; rate-limiting is per-process, allowing 4× the intended traffic per IP.
antipattern.root-cause: Author migrated from 1.8 config without removing `nbproc` and switching to `nbthread`.
antipattern.fix: Replace `nbproc N` with `nbthread N` + `cpu-map auto:1/1-N 0-(N-1)`. Stick-tables now live in a single shared address space and rate limits become accurate.

antipattern.symptom: Primary HAProxy crashes. DNS TTL is 60s; corporate DNS resolvers cache for 5+ minutes; mobile carriers cache for hours. Some users see service down for 30 minutes.
antipattern.root-cause: Operators relied on DNS round-robin or low-TTL records for failover; no VIP + VRRP in place.
antipattern.fix: Deploy keepalived on master + backup nodes with VRRP and a `track_script` checking HAProxy health. Move clients to the VIP; DNS now resolves to a static highly-available address.
